channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study reveals presence of contaminants and drugs in sharks near tourist areas of the Bahamas
item.description: A scientific study published in the journal *Environmental Pollution* has found traces of cocaine, caffeine, painkillers, and antidepressants in several shark species living off the coast of the Baham...

item.title: AI-generated song about police killer provokes public outrage
item.description: A recently released AI-generated song praising Dezi Freeman, a convicted police killer, has ignited widespread criticism and controversy across social media and news outlets. The song, created using a...

item.title: PM Modi explains benefits of expanding Lok Sabha seats under women's reservation plan
item.description: In a recent statement, Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasised that the women’s reservation policy, which seeks to allocate one-third of seats in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies to women, will bene...

item.title: Pate Begins Talks with Airbus on Nigeria's New Health Emergency System
item.description: Professor Muhammad Ali Pate, Nigeria's Coordinating Minister of Health and Social Welfare, has initiated discussions with Airbus, Europe’s leading aerospace corporation, for the procurement of emergen...

item.title: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's Controversial Drama Explores Gun Violence and Trust Issues
item.description: In 'The Drama', Zendaya and Robert Pattinson star in a dark, provocative film about a couple on the brink of marriage. Directed by Kristoffer Borgli, the film delves into a seemingly perfect relations...

item.title: Lawmakers scrutinize Mamdani’s nominee for investigative post over independence concerns
item.description: A recent piece from Politico examines new political scrutiny surrounding the nominee selected by New York Assemblymember Zohran Mamdani for a key investigative position. The nominee, whose name was ad...

item.title: Sykes prepares for his first solo concert and builds his independent label
item.description: South African musician Sykes, whose real name is Ayabonga Zuma, has revealed plans to host his own solo concert while continuing to grow his music empire through his independent label, Lekelela Entert...

item.title: Kenya Power schedules planned maintenance causing power cuts in five counties on April 7
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has informed the public about a planned electricity shutdown in parts of five counties on Tuesday, April 7, 2026. The company said this temporary outage will al...

item.title: Arnel Pineda opens up about wanting to leave Journey amid personal struggles
item.description: Filipino singer Arnel Pineda, best known as the frontman of the legendary American rock band Journey, recently revealed that he once considered leaving the group because of personal problems. In his i...

item.title: Veteran Ladysmith Black Mambazo member Albert Mazibuko dies at 77 after decades of musical legacy
item.description: Albert Mazibuko, a long-standing member of the iconic South African musical group Ladysmith Black Mambazo, has passed away at the age of 77. The group’s manager, Xolani Majozi, confirmed the news on M...

item.title: Moi University Student Drowns at Kesses River, Family Prepares Burial in Kisii
item.description: A tragic incident occurred at Moi University in Eldoret when a third-year student, Douglas Ayienda, drowned while swimming in the swollen Kesses River near the university’s waterfall. The accident hap...

item.title: FGC Kano alumni protest Federal Government plan to allocate school land for private real estate development
item.description: The Federal Government College Kano Old Students Association (FGCKOSA) don raise strong objection against the Federal Government decision wey wan give out 30 hectares of the school land to one private...

item.title: Trump Issues New Warnings to Iran Over Deadline Compliance
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump has escalated his warnings towards Iran, stating that the country will face severe consequences if it fails to meet his specified deadline. The remarks reflect ongoi...

item.title: Teen surfer recounts shark bite scare at Middleton Beach, South Australia
item.description: Sixteen-year-old surfer Oliver Tokic-Bensley narrowly escaped a shark bite incident while surfing at Middleton Beach, about 80 kilometres south of Adelaide, on Good Friday. Having been on his surfboar...

item.title: Space Force Seeks Over $8 Billion for Advanced Space-Based Targeting Systems
item.description: The U.S. Space Force's fiscal year 2027 budget request signals a significant investment in space-based surveillance capabilities. The service is seeking more than $7 billion in procurement for Air Mov...

item.title: Road Safety Education Proposed After Tragic Easter Toll
item.description: Australia is reeling from a devastating Easter period on its roads, with at least 14 lives lost by Monday lunchtime, and figures from Western Australia still to be finalised. The grim statistics highl...

item.title: PM Modi hails success of Kalpakkam reactor achieving criticality
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed his pride over the Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor in Tamil Nadu reaching a major milestone by attaining criticality. This achievement is a significant step for I...

item.title: Parliament resists Ruto’s push for tax waivers and special privileges to foreign firm
item.description: In a dramatic show of independence, Members of Parliament went against President William Ruto’s instructions to approve sweeping tax waivers and diplomatic immunities for a foreign-owned company eyein...

item.title: Wike Explains Controversial Statement Directed At Journalist Seun Okinbaloye
item.description: The FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike, don clarify say the talk wey e give about shooting Channels TV news anchor, Seun Okinbaloye, na just figurative language. This talk come after Okinbaloye yan for the ‘Po...

item.title: Nairobi nutritionist quits stable job after seminar, starts successful clinic in Mlolongo with one employee after five months
item.description: Keruchi Derrick, a young nutritionist based in Nairobi, made a bold decision to quit his well-paying job five months ago. Despite earning a decent salary, he felt drained by long working hours, tight ...
